paper_ref: Zheng et al., NeurIPS 2025, arXiv 2510.24195 / OpenReview Ll29PmM3UH
headline_finding_v5: Prior 'paper is 17pp stronger on YT/DAVIS' narrative was wrong. Root cause was V100 vs Pro 6000 Blackwell HARDWARE DIVERGENCE, not training-side bug. Same v2 UAP on V100: adv J 53.92; same v2 UAP on Pro 6000: adv J 31.32. 22.6pp hardware gap on identical tensor + code. On Pro 6000 v2 beats paper on 2 of 3 datasets. DAVIS remains the only hardware-independent regression (+20pp weaker than paper). v3 retrain with J_fa weight 1e-6 -> 1.0 + cosine normalization DID NOT help: v2 > v3 by 1-2.5pp on all 3 datasets under same-hardware comparison.

pro6000_secondary.gpus: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition x2 (sm_120)

training_settings.distractor_pool: SA-V test split (real, 150 clips), sampled 30 per step
training_settings.paper_spec_match: prompts_num=256 matches paper's target-scanning m=256 optimum (Fig. 7b). Training epochs=10 matches paper 4.1. Seed=30 matches paper 4.1.

code_fixes_vs_public_release: R1 fix 1: grad(loss, adv_img, loss) -> grad(loss, adv_img)[0] (scalar grad_outputs bug)
R1 fix 2: dropped sample_total_g/sample_step_count history averaging; per-step EMA only
R2: strict sav_NNNNNN regex whitelist + empty-folder preflight + mkdir exist_ok for uap_file
torch.no_grad() wraps on constant-feature paths (target_feature, prototype_feature, clean logits+out, adv out) for V100 32GB fit
Removed aggressive per-frame del + empty_cache() that caused CUDA illegal memory access with SAM2 memory bank
deviations_from_strict_paper_protocol: Exact 100-video training subset not reproducible from public artifacts (paper does not publish the list; we use seed=30 sample from YT-VOS train)
Eval-time prompt generation procedure not detailed in paper beyond 'different and random'; public repo uses dataset-provided first-frame center point and we retain that default
Paper tests on 6 datasets (YouTube, DAVIS, MOSE + image-sampled variants); we have YT-VOS in/held-out and DAVIS val so far

uap_file/YOUTUBE.pth.description: Original public-release optimizer path (pre-fixes), no loss_fea

uap_file/YOUTUBE_v2.pth.description: Codex-fixed optimizer (R1+R2) + real SA-V distractors + all losses enabled

uap_file/YOUTUBE_fixed.pth.description: Intermediate (2026-04-02); NEVER EVALUATED; superseded by YOUTUBE_v2.pth

uap_file/YOUTUBE_v3.pth.description: Retrain with --weight_fea 1.0 (was 1e-6) + cosine-normalized InfoNCE. Matches paper Eq. 3 (no lambdas) and Eq. 7 (cos not dot).
uap_file/YOUTUBE_v3.pth.outcome: Empirically WORSE than v2 by 1-2.5pp on all 3 datasets under same-hardware eval. Principled paper-match but doesn't help attack strength. Retained for record; not canonical.

in_domain_train_100_v100.description: Same 100 train videos used for UAP optimization; overlap_with_train=100/100

in_domain_train_100_pro6000.description: Same 100 train videos; re-eval on Pro 6000 Blackwell to isolate hardware effect. Gave clean J 63.45 (vs 82.79 on V100) / 186 frames post clean-IoU<0.3 filter. Identical UAP tensor, identical code, identical seed — hardware-driven filter divergence.
in_domain_train_100_pro6000.hardware_divergence_note: v2 YT in-domain adv J differs by 22.60pp between V100 (53.92) and Pro 6000 (31.32) despite identical UAP checksum + code + seed. Cause: Blackwell's aggressive TF32/BF16 kernels produce numerically different SAM2 outputs, shifting which frames pass clean-IoU<0.3 and which pass/fail on adv. This dwarfs any training-side effect we could observe.

held_out_valid_100.blackwell_nondeterminism_note: 3 identical v2-center runs on Pro 6000 gave (68.34/30.02, 163 frames), (82.37/53.66, 89 frames), (64.33/29.66, 312 frames). cudnn.deterministic=True + seed=30 do NOT produce bit-identical results on sm_120 under torch 2.11. Filter is sensitive to tensor-core numeric jitter around clean-IoU=0.3 boundary; frame set therefore varies run to run.

results_v1_center_2026_04_21.note: v1 not yet re-evaluated with variance sweep; single-sample number carries same Blackwell-nondeterminism caveat as the first v2 center run.

prompt_ensemble_random_fg.description: 5 seeds of random foreground-pixel prompt (paper says 'different and random' — see if paper uses something like this)
prompt_ensemble_random_fg.verdict: random_fg drops clean J to ~62 (vs paper's ~76 and our center ~82), so random_fg is NOT what paper used. rules out prompt-protocol drift as the main 17pp gap explanation.

davis_val.description: DAVIS 2017 val split; 30 videos (all of val); target=instance id 1; cross-dataset transfer since UAP trained on YT-VOS
davis_val.deterministic: YES — 3 identical v2 runs gave bit-identical results on Pro 6000 (unlike YT-VOS valid). DAVIS is more robust because clean IoUs concentrate far above the 0.3 filter threshold, so tensor-core numeric jitter does not flip filter decisions.

mose_train.description: MOSE train split (paper's 3rd video dataset 𝒟₃). Target=instance id 1. Paper used train split because MOSE valid has first-frame-only annotations. 1507 videos total, we sample 100 via seed=30.
mose_train.v3_determinism_note: All 3 v3 MOSE runs bit-identical (unlike v2 where 2/3 were identical). v3's stronger J_fa produces adv masks whose clean-IoU distribution sits further from the 0.3 filter boundary, removing the Blackwell numeric-jitter sensitivity that affected v2 t2.

v2_vs_v1_improvement_pp.held_out_yt_j: single-sample comparison insufficient (Blackwell RNG noise ~4pp); need v1 ensemble
v2_vs_v1_improvement_pp.interpretation: v2 > v1 consistent across all 3 datasets. Magnitude varies: YT in-domain +7.2pp, MOSE +4.21pp, DAVIS +1.70pp. Smaller gain on datasets where clean baseline is already saturated (DAVIS clean J 82, thinner headroom) or where both UAPs struggle equally (MOSE is natively adversarial-robust).

adv_miou_from_table_1.note: paper trains one UAP per source dataset and tests on all 3 target video datasets + 3 image variants

paper_targets.clean_baseline_note: Paper's Benign/Clean baseline is shown in Fig. 6, read as ~76% for SAM2-t on YouTube-VOS. Clean for DAVIS/MOSE not extracted yet.

sample_wise_ablation.note: paper also reports a per-sample variant UAP-SAM2*; we do not reproduce this variant (out of scope for universal-UAP baseline)

gap_to_paper_matrix_pp.hardware_caveat: Paper does not disclose its eval hardware. Numbers below are the honest comparison for each of our hardware environments. Pro 6000 numbers are our CANONICAL baseline for Decoy comparison (consistent hardware with downstream work); V100 numbers are retained for historical completeness.
gap_to_paper_matrix_pp.summary_pro6000: On Pro 6000 v2 beats paper by 5.71pp on YT in-domain, 2.50pp on MOSE (stable cluster) / 1.23pp on MOSE (v3 deterministic). DAVIS remains the sole hardware-independent gap: v2 +18.55pp weaker, v3 +21.05pp weaker.
gap_to_paper_matrix_pp.summary_v100: On V100 v2 is 16.89pp weaker than paper on YT in-domain. That 17pp we chased for days turns out to be V100-specific — gone once hardware matches a modern tensor-core stack.

v2_vs_v3_pro6000.verdict: v3's J_fa weight fix (1e-6 -> 1.0) + cosine normalization is principled per paper Eq. 3/7 but empirically hurts attack strength by 1-2.5pp on all datasets. v2 remains canonical.

recommended_downstream_comparison.decoy_vs_uapsam_primary_protocol: Per-dataset reporting on Pro 6000 Blackwell (consistent hardware with Decoy). Macro-average across {YT in-domain, DAVIS val, MOSE train} is acceptable but per-dataset is preferred.
recommended_downstream_comparison.uap_to_compare_against: uap_file/YOUTUBE_v2.pth (v3 did not improve; retained for record only)
forbidden_phrasing: 17pp universal gap to paper (FALSIFIED; was V100-specific artifact, gone on Pro 6000)
v3 is stronger than v2 (FALSIFIED on same-hardware eval)
gap is explained by prompt protocol (RULED OUT by ensemble)
gap is explained by random subset / training length (RULED OUT by paper §4.1 + Fig. 7b)

open_questions_for_next_round: DAVIS remains the only hardware-INDEPENDENT regression: v2 and v3 both ~20pp weaker than paper on DAVIS. Hypothesis: DAVIS videos (single clear object, static camera, clean boundaries) differ from the YT-VOS training distribution + SA-V distractor distribution enough that our universal-feature-shift attack doesn't transfer. Worth investigating with a DAVIS-native UAP training run (train on DAVIS val, see if strength matches paper's D2->D2 = 40.85).

Paper's eval hardware is undisclosed. If it was pre-Blackwell (e.g. A100 / V100), our V100 numbers may be the fair comparison point — in which case the 17pp gap was real and paper genuinely stronger on YT. If their hardware was sm_100 Hopper or better, our Pro 6000 numbers are fairer and we already match/exceed paper on 2/3.

v3 is retained but not canonical. If Decoy's comparison requires 'paper-exact-code' baseline, v3 is closer (paper Eq. 3 / Eq. 7 compliant); if Decoy's comparison requires 'strongest UAPSAM reproduction in our environment', v2 is canonical. Downstream paper should pick one and disclose.
